item.title: It's time for yet another unhinged XL Wednesday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
looks so nice
copiers
wonder what race he would pick in Skyrim
that is only a million times cooler than my childhood bunk bed
i don't like to work no matter what the weather is
usually race car or top hat
this might be Tom Cruise's best movie for me
mmm hmm
ok sure but white chocolate? not saying they are bad but..
that's not real is it?
fair but why is dude pointing a gun at MJ?
as is tradition
i saw infinity fireflies the other night
tough decisions
i want that
finger lickin' good
that's all for me.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: Brian Cox explains Einstein's theory of relativity in under 3 mins.
item.description: Plus, he uses Jim Al-Khalili (one of our greatest living theoretical physicists) as a prop!
item.dc:creator: mercbrit

item.title: I keep seeing this horrible game in the Imgur ads
item.description: There's enough real misery in the world that to profit from it in this way seems utterly contemptible. Whoever came up with this should spend a year in poverty themselves. Edit: Not "most viral" but I just wanted to add that I have learned a lot from the comments here; a) that I'm not the only one who hates these ads, and b) I learned of the existence of "whales." What a crazy world we live in. Edit 2: Ouch, MV was totally unexpected for this one. I was mostly just "reee"ing into the void, but I seem to have struck a nerve. Thanks to all who have educated me on the economics and logistics of these deceptive ads and absurd games. PS: For those commenting about uBlock and "there are ads?" etc, I use adblockers but do most of my Imguring on mobile, where I have found no such niceties.
item.dc:creator: AyatollahBahloni

item.dc:creator: MrsPrendsen

item.title: Hugh Jackman eating a brazilian 'coxinha' for the first time.
item.description:
Coxinhas are roughly equivalent to croquettes, but smoother and made with chicken, sometimes with cheese.
Needless to say, Brazilians loved his reaction. But brazilians loved him already since...
He met up and paid a homage to Isaac Bardavid, who basically voiced Wolverine in Brazil and made part of a huge chunk of our childhood. EDIT: Thanks for the upvotes everyone! Remember, to eat healthy! I do some reviews as a hobby, if you wanna check them, feel free to visit https://hypeimmune.wordpress.com/
item.dc:creator: Skullwriter

item.title: What to expect when you get a puppy
item.dc:creator: tampacl

item.title: The Wilhelm Scream
item.description:
I thought this was pretty cool.
The actor who did the scream is Sheb Wooley. Wooley was also country western musician and a popular actor in many old west movies and serials. He's most famous for the novelty hit "The Purple People Eater," and for his recurring character on the show "Rawhide." In the late 1940s, when Wooley was breaking into the industry, he found work doing stock audio effects for Thomas Valentino. The Valentino Sound Effects Library became the go-to for many film and television productions for decades, which is why so many productions had access to Wooley's recorded scream.
The first use of the Wilhelm Scream in a 1951 movie, “Distant Drums," starring Gary Cooper. The film was only moderately successful and quickly forgotten.
The scream was used for the third time in 1953's "The Charge at Feather River." The scream is called the "Wilhelm Scream" after the character Private Wilhelm — the above clip pretty much explains why. "The Charge at Feather River" was reasonably profitable but not actually any good, and these days is remembered primarily for the "Wilhelm Scream."
Sheb Wooley made for a believable cowboy because he was an actual cowboy. He grew up on a farm in Erick, Oklahoma, competed in rodeos while still in high school, and drove cattle in Montana when he wasn't working in Hollywood. Aside from "Rawhide," he had credits in classics like "High Noon," "Giant," "Hoosiers," "The Outaw Josey Wales," and "Silverado," one of my favorite westerns.
♥️
item.dc:creator: softballguy

item.title: Professional - someone who devote their entire life to a specific practice.
item.description: Doesn't matter if it is cooking, medicine, engineering, or law. You are a professional, or you have a profession, if you are dedicating your life to a single craft.
